questions.question: By noon, I {a1} for very long.

a1.verb_hint: Use will not have been + verb-ing.

variants: By noon, I {a1} for very long.

questions.question: By Friday, she {a1} here for a month.

a1.verb_hint: Won't can replace will not.

variants: By Friday, she {a1} here for a month.

questions.question: By then, they {a1} for two hours.

a1.verb_hint: The negative duration reaches a future point.

variants: By then, they {a1} for two hours.

questions.question: By next year, we {a1} on it long enough.

a1.verb_hint: Not comes after will.

variants: By next year, we {a1} on it long enough.

questions.question: By six, Tom {a1} for four hours; he starts at five.

a1.verb_hint: He may be studying then, but not for four hours yet.

variants: By six, Tom {a1} for four hours; he starts at five.

variants: By Monday, Marta {a1} the course for long.

variants: By tonight, they {a1} all day.

variants: By the deadline, I {a1} on the report for a week.
